title: 2026 Nevada gubernatorial election
description: Nevada will elect its next governor in 2026, with the primary scheduled for June 9 and the general election on November 3. The race is a key test in a closely divided swing state where the governor will influence housing affordability, taxes, education, public safety, and reproductive rights. Incumbent Republican Joe Lombardo is seeking reelection, and Democratic Attorney General Aaron Ford is the best-known challenger; Washoe County Commission Chair Alexis Hill is also running, along with other candidates.

candidates.name: Joe Lombardo
candidates.summary: Joe Lombardo is the Republican incumbent governor of Nevada and a former Clark County sheriff. He was elected governor in 2022 and is seeking reelection in 2026, with his public profile centered on public safety, education, and economic development.

career_history.title: Sheriff
career_history.description: Elected sheriff of Clark County in 2014 and re-elected in 2018.

career_history.title: Assistant sheriff and other ranks
career_history.description: Served for 26 years in LVMPD, rising through multiple ranks.

career_history.title: Soldier
career_history.organization: United States Army and Nevada National Guard
career_history.description: Served in the U.S. Army and Nevada National Guard after college.

voting_record.bill_description: Joe Lombardo is governor and does not have a legislative roll-call voting record.

top_donors.name: Southern Nevada Building & Construction Trades Council
NV Republican Party Central Committee Non-Federal Victory

Healthcare.stance: Lombardo says he supports access to contraceptives as part of healthcare and says he will work to keep them accessible. He also says he is pro-life, but that Nevada voters should decide abortion law and that he does not support an abortion ban in Nevada.

sources.title: Reproductive Health — Joe Lombardo for Governor
A note from Joe Lombardo — Joe Lombardo for Governor

Economy.stance: Lombardo supports a diversified, pro‑business Nevada economy built on tax relief, reduced red tape, and targeted incentives to grow high‑tech, manufacturing, and energy sectors, paired with robust workforce development through private‑sector partnerships. He advocates policies like child‑care tax credits and targeted abatements to broaden the economy beyond gaming and tourism.

sources.title: Lombardo economic development bill targets child care, high-tech business, rural housing - The Nevada Independent
Taxes & The Economy — Joe Lombardo for Governor
Governor Lombardo Advances Nevada’s Top Priorities During Washington, D.C. Visit

Climate/Energy.stance: Lombardo has not published a detailed climate platform in the sources reviewed. His public materials emphasize economic development and state governance rather than specific climate or energy policy proposals.

sources.title: Joe Lombardo, Governor of Nevada
Joe Lombardo for Governor

Reproductive Rights.stance: Signed SB 131 in 2023 to protect abortion access for out-of-state patients and in-state providers in Nevada; he has stated that abortion policy should be decided by Nevadans at the ballot and that he does not support an abortion ban in Nevada.

sources.title: Nevada Republican governor approves abortion protections in cross-party move - AP News
Nevada governor signs new abortion protections into law - NBC News
Nevada's GOP governor approves abortion protections - Los Angeles Times

Immigration.stance: Governor Lombardo says Nevada will follow federal immigration law, will not be a sanctuary state, and will collaborate with federal authorities on immigration enforcement, including removing violent or repeat offenders from communities swiftly and safely.

sources.title: DOJ and State of Nevada Sign Immigration Enforcement Agreement
Justice Department Announces Memorandum of Understanding with Nevada to Collaborate on Immigration
Nevada First State to Be Removed From List of Sanctuary Jurisdictions

Guns & Safety.stance: Lombardo affirms the Second Amendment, opposes gun registries, and his campaign has signaled an effort to repeal Nevada’s red-flag law; he has also vetoed multiple gun-control bills, including a ghost-gun ban and a measure prohibiting guns at polling places, while supporting targeted safety measures.

sources.title: Does Joe Lombardo want to repeal Nevada's red flag law?
Why Did Nevada's Governor Veto a Ban on Ghost Guns?

Foreign Policy.stance: Publicly regards Iran as a long-time threat to the United States and Israel; states that Iran must never obtain a nuclear weapon. In the wake of the U.S.-Israel strikes, Lombardo called for a swift resolution and said Nevada law enforcement would coordinate with federal partners, with the possibility of increased police presence at certain locations to ensure public safety.

sources.title: Governor Joe Lombardo addresses U.S. attack on Iran
Lombardo says ‘certain locations’ could see increased police presence amid strikes on Iran

Social Justice.stance: Lombardo publicly backs a 2026 ballot initiative (Protect Girls’ Sports) to amend the Nevada Constitution to bar transgender athletes from participating in girls’ and women’s sports, arguing it protects fairness and safety; critics say the measure would discriminate against transgender students.

sources.title: Lombardo leads 2026 ballot initiative to block students born male from female sports
Judge rules Lombardo's trans athlete ballot initiative can proceed
Nevada governor backs ballot initiative restricting transgender athletes in school sports
Nevada governor backs 'Protect Girls' Sports' initiative petition targeting trans athletes

Education.stance: Joe Lombardo remains focused on expanding school choice and accountability in Nevada's education system. In January 2026, he announced that Nevada will opt into the Federal Tax Credit Scholarship Program to broaden school choice (SGO tax credits), complementing his push to grow charter schools, support opportunity scholarships, and invest in Education Savings Accounts and literacy initiatives.

sources.title: Governor Lombardo Opts-In To Federal Tax Credit Scholarship Program
Lombardo joins Nevada education leaders highlight charter school growth
Education — Joe Lombardo for Governor

Tech & AI.stance: Signed SB 263 in 2025 to expand Nevada's definition of child pornography to include AI-generated or AI-modified images, demonstrating a policy action to regulate AI-generated explicit content and aid enforcement; no comprehensive tech/AI platform published, but the record shows active AI-related legislation.

Election Reform.stance: Lombardo maintains that election administration should be a state issue and opposes efforts to nationalize elections; he has also advocated for commonsense reforms such as voter ID to strengthen election integrity.

sources.title: IndyTalks: Lombardo pushes against nationalizing elections, calls it 'state issue'
I promised Nevadans commonsense election reforms like Voter ID
Joe Lombardo on election reforms including Voter ID

Local Issues.stance: Public-safety policy has been a central Local Issues focus, notably the signing of Assembly Bill 4, the Nevada Safe Streets and Neighborhoods Act, a comprehensive crime-package that strengthens protections for stalking and domestic violence victims, increases penalties for crimes against children and seniors, boosts safety in schools and the resort corridor, and expands DUI enforcement; AB 4 took effect January 1, 2026. Lombardo frames this as putting victims first and giving law enforcement the tools to keep communities safe.

sources.title: Governor Lombardo Ceremonially Signs Nevada Safe Streets and Neighborhoods Act
Gov. Lombardo signs Nevada Safe Streets and Neighborhoods Act
Nevada governor signs expansive crime bill in Las Vegas
Governor Lombardo's signature 'tough on crime' bill goes into effect start of 2026

candidates.donor_summary: OpenSecrets donor-search results indicate Lombardo’s fundraising includes Nevada business and gaming interests, but the accessible pages do not provide a complete top-industry breakdown or total raised for the race. The available donor pages show contributions from local real-estate and business entities, so the donor picture is partial rather than comprehensive.
candidates.voting_summary: Joe Lombardo does not have a legislative roll-call voting record because he serves as governor rather than as a legislator. His public record is instead defined by executive actions such as signing and vetoing bills, which are tracked in state records and by Vote Smart.

links.title: Joseph Lombardo - Vote Smart - Facts For All
OpenSecrets donor search results for Joe Lombardo
OpenSecrets donor search results for Joe Lombardo

candidates.name: Aaron Ford
candidates.summary: Aaron Ford is Nevada’s attorney general and a Democratic candidate for governor in 2026. He previously served in the Nevada State Senate and has centered his campaign on affordability, housing, health care, energy, consumer protection, and public safety.

summary_sources.title: About — Aaron Ford for Nevada Governor
AG Aaron Ford - Nevada Attorney General

career_history.title: State Senator
career_history.description: Served as a state senator and held majority leader and minority leader roles.

career_history.title: Partner
career_history.description: Worked in private practice as a partner.

career_history.title: Partner
career_history.description: Worked in private practice as a partner.

career_history.title: Public school math teacher
career_history.description: Taught math before entering law and politics.

voting_record.bill_description: Aaron Ford is Nevada attorney general and does not have a legislative roll-call voting record.

source.title: Aaron D Ford Top Contributors - OpenSecrets
Aaron D Ford Top Contributors - OpenSecrets
Aaron D Ford Top Contributors - OpenSecrets
Aaron D Ford Top Contributors - OpenSecrets
Aaron D Ford Top Contributors - OpenSecrets

Healthcare.stance: [To be updated if new information found]

Economy.stance: Ford’s updated economy position centers on making Nevada more affordable by advancing his Affordable Nevada housing agenda, which cracks down on corporate homebuying, caps security deposits at one month’s rent, bans junk fees, and expands rental and down payment assistance to lower housing costs. He also supports using federal land for affordable housing and pledges to challenge tariffs that raise costs for Nevadans, pairing these measures with pro-labor, pro-worker economic policies.

sources.title: Aaron Ford Unveils First Plank of Affordable Nevada Agenda: Housing
Ford intros housing plan with rental aid programs, crackdown on corporate homebuying

Climate/Energy.stance: Ford supports defending and maintaining the 2009 Greenhouse Gas Endangerment Finding and related federal emissions standards, joining a multi-state coalition to challenge EPA’s unlawful rescission in order to protect public health and climate protections grounded in statute, precedent, and science.

sources.title: Attorney General Ford Challenges Unlawful Recission of Landmark 2009 Greenhouse Gas Endangerment Finding
AG Ford sues Trump administration over fossil fuel emissions regulation
Ford sues Trump to reverse rescinding 2009 Greenhouse Gas Endangerment Finding

Reproductive Rights.stance: Ford has continued to defend reproductive rights in 2026, joining a coalition of 22 attorneys general to challenge Trump-era regulations and defend no-cost contraception under the ACA; he has publicly defended access to birth control and contraception care in Nevada, reinforcing his track record on reproductive health.

sources.title: Attorney General Ford Defends Access to Birth Control and Other Contraceptive Care
Attorney General Ford joins coalition to defend access to contraceptive care

Immigration.stance: Ford maintains that he does not support sanctuary policies. In 2025 his office published Model Immigration Policies mandated by the Nevada Legislature to guide law enforcement, courts, and higher education; he says these policies are not intended to create a sanctuary state and are aimed at enhancing public safety.

sources.title: Attorney General Aaron Ford Releases Response to Governor Lombardo’s Misleading Statement on Model Immigration Policies
Attorney General Ford Announces Publication of Model Immigration Policies

Guns & Safety.stance: Ford’s public record and campaign materials emphasize public safety and efforts to reduce gun violence, including support for laws and litigation aimed at keeping dangerous weapons and devices out of circulation. The sources reviewed do not provide a detailed campaign gun-policy page, but they show him backing measures to protect communities and oppose broad access for dangerous actors.

sources.title: Aaron Ford for Nevada Governor
Attorney General Ford Sues Trump Administration to Prevent Efforts to Distribute Thousands of Machine Gun Devices Across Country
Aaron Ford - Giffords.org

Foreign Policy.stance: Ford has not published a formal foreign policy platform for Nevada; his public actions have centered on opposing federal policy actions (such as tariffs and ICE enforcement), indicating opposition to federal overreach but no developed foreign policy doctrine.

sources.title: Attorney General Ford Seeks Court Order to Stop the Trump Administration’s Illegal Tariffs
Aaron Ford Statement on ICE’s Federal Overreach Across The Nation

Social Justice.stance: Ford continues to defend LGBTQ+ rights and anti-discrimination protections, and has actively joined multistate efforts to ban conversion therapy for minors, defend transgender service members, and address hate crime and MMIP issues. He has also engaged in actions to uphold fair housing protections by opposing rollbacks to the Fair Housing Act regulations.

sources.title: Attorney General Ford Joins Multistate Fight to Protect Youth from Conversion ‘Therapy’
Attorney General Ford Stands with Transgender Military Members in Challenge to President Trump’s Discriminatory Executive Order
Attorney General Ford Leads Coalition of Attorneys General in Urging Congress to Pass Tribal Warrant Fairness Act
Attorney General Ford Announces Inaugural Meeting of Nevada Hate Crimes Task Force
Attorney General Ford Joins Coalition Opposing Rollback of Crucial Fair Housing Regulations

Education.stance: Ford continues to prioritize education funding, higher teacher pay, and expanded supports, including universal pre-K and other student-services; his campaign has recently highlighted guaranteeing school meals for all Nevada students and ongoing educator engagement through education roundtables to shape policy.

sources.title: Aaron Ford for Nevada Governor
Aaron Ford Holds Education Roundtable with Southern Nevada Educators, Parents, and Students

Tech & AI.stance: Ford emphasizes strengthening cyber security and consumer protection in the digital space, including collaboration with federal and local partners to combat cyber-enabled crime. Nevada’s Attorney General’s Office joined the FBI-led Las Vegas Cyber Task Force to enhance investigations into online fraud and related offenses; no AI-specific regulatory platform has been publicly released.

sources.title: Attorney General Ford Announces Nevada Has Joined Cyber Task Force
Aaron Ford for Nevada Governor

Election Reform.stance: Ford’s campaign materials reviewed here do not show a specific election-reform plank. His public campaign focus is on affordability, housing, public safety, cost of living, education, and jobs, so no clear stated position on election reform was found in the sources reviewed.

sources.title: Aaron Ford for Nevada Governor

Local Issues.stance: Ford’s updated housing plan, “Affordable Nevada,” targets lowering costs by capping security deposits to one month’s rent, banning junk fees, cracking down on corporate landlords and price-fixing, expanding rental and down payment assistance, and expediting permitting while leveraging federal land for affordable housing. The plan does not include broad rent control, though Ford has indicated openness to researching and discussing rent-control options further.

sources.title: Ford intros housing plan with rental aid programs, crackdown on corporate homebuying
Projects partly funded by governor's housing bill take off, aiming at affordability crisis
Aaron Ford Unveils First Plank of Affordable Nevada Agenda: Housing
Ford, Hill promise to prioritize housing if elected Governor

candidates.donor_summary: OpenSecrets shows Aaron D. Ford raised $3,436,837 in 2022 from 3,698 itemized individual contributions, with a large share coming from organizations unavailable in the public record and notable disclosed donors including Wynn Resorts, Grant & Eisenhofer, Hollis Public Affairs, IBEW, IUPAT, Everytown for Gun Safety, Fidelity National Financial, and Nevada’s Future PAC. The top industries were lawyers/law firms, casinos/gambling, health services/HMOs, and tribal governments, indicating a coalition of legal, gaming, health-care, labor, and advocacy money. Confidence: high. Source URLs: https://www.opensecrets.org/officeholders/aaron-d-ford/contributors?cycle=2022&id=6508433 ; https://www.opensecrets.org/officeholders/aaron-d-ford/summary?cycle=2023&id=6508433
candidates.voting_summary: Aaron Ford served in the Nevada State Senate from 2013 to 2019, so he does have a legislative voting record. His public profile and campaign materials emphasize his later work as attorney general on consumer protection, reproductive rights, housing affordability, and public safety.

links.title: Aaron D Ford Top Contributors - OpenSecrets
Aaron D Ford Money Profile - OpenSecrets
AG Aaron Ford - Nevada Attorney General
Aaron Ford - Vote Smart - Facts For All

candidates.name: Matthew Winterhawk
candidates.summary: Matthew Winterhawk is a Republican candidate for governor of Nevada in 2026. His campaign describes him as a father, husband, business operator, nonprofit founder, and civic reformer, and Ballotpedia says he was born in Kansas City, Kansas, earned a high school diploma from Benson Union High School, attended Johnson County Community College, and worked in nonprofit and business roles.

summary_sources.title: About Matthew Winterhawk | Nevada Governor Candidate 2026

voting_record.bill_description: Matthew Winterhawk is a gubernatorial candidate with no identified legislative roll-call voting record.

Healthcare.stance: Winterhawk’s published platform does not lay out a broad healthcare plan, but his NPPA materials say parents should have direct control over children’s healthcare and that medical, social, or psychological intervention in children should not occur without parental consent.

sources.title: NPPA - Matthew Winterhawk for Governor of Nevada (2026)
Top 6 Issues for Nevada Voters | Winterhawk on Education, Safety, Housing, Elections

Economy.stance: Winterhawk says he would create a Department of Government Efficiency to audit agencies, track tax dollars, and reduce waste. His campaign frames this as part of a broader effort to improve transparency, accountability, and conditions for small businesses and local ownership.

sources.title: Matthew Winterhawk - Ballotpedia
First 100 Days as Governor | Winterhawk Executive Action Plan
How I'll Run the Executive Branch

Climate/Energy.stance: Winterhawk’s public materials emphasize Nevada state control over land and water rather than a detailed climate or energy agenda. He supports Nevada-controlled lands and resources, auditing federal water contracts, and prioritizing drought mitigation and conservation at the state level.

sources.title: Top 6 Issues for Nevada Voters | Winterhawk on Education, Safety, Housing, Elections
Nevada Land Sovereignty and Reclamation Act (NLSARA)
Matthew Winterhawk - Ballotpedia

Reproductive Rights.stance: Winterhawk’s reviewed materials do not state a direct position on abortion. His NPPA materials focus on parental rights and require parental consent for medical or social interventions involving minors.

sources.title: NPPA - Matthew Winterhawk for Governor of Nevada (2026)
Top 6 Issues for Nevada Voters | Winterhawk on Education, Safety, Housing, Elections

Immigration.stance: Winterhawk says Nevada’s laws, schools, hospitals, and public services should prioritize legal residents, citizens, and people here lawfully. He opposes sanctuary policies, supports secure ID for voting and benefits, and calls for stronger coordination with sheriffs and federal agencies to remove criminal offenders through proper channels.

sources.title: Top 6 Issues for Nevada Voters | Winterhawk on Education, Safety, Housing, Elections
NLSARA - Matthew Winterhawk for Governor of Nevada (2026)

Guns & Safety.stance: Winterhawk’s public issue page emphasizes crime prevention and public safety, including zero tolerance for violent and repeat offenders, stronger penalties for organized retail crime and trafficking, and better resources for victims. The reviewed sources do not show a specific firearms-policy statement.

sources.title: Top 6 Issues for Nevada Voters | Winterhawk on Education, Safety, Housing, Elections

Foreign Policy.stance: Winterhawk’s reviewed materials do not present a conventional foreign policy platform. His public agenda focuses on Nevada sovereignty and state control over land and resources rather than foreign-policy-specific positions.

sources.title: NLSARA - Matthew Winterhawk for Governor of Nevada (2026)
Top 6 Issues for Nevada Voters | Winterhawk on Education, Safety, Housing, Elections

Social Justice.stance: Winterhawk’s platform emphasizes education accountability, parental rights, public safety, election integrity, and transparency in government rather than a broad social-justice agenda. On immigration, he prioritizes legal residents and supports robust voting safeguards.

sources.title: Matthew Winterhawk - Ballotpedia
Top 6 Issues for Nevada Voters | Winterhawk on Education, Safety, Housing, Elections

Education.stance: Winterhawk says Nevada classrooms should teach skills, literacy, critical thinking, and civics rather than political or corporate agendas. He supports parental rights, curriculum transparency, age-appropriate materials, and restoring civics, literacy, trades, law, and constitutional education.

sources.title: Top 6 Issues for Nevada Voters | Winterhawk on Education, Safety, Housing, Elections
Matthew Winterhawk - Ballotpedia

Tech & AI.stance: Winterhawk’s NIDDA proposal would require apps and websites to disclose data collection, ban sale or transfer of biometric data without explicit consent, create a Nevada data privacy agency, and label ads that use behavioral tracking or AI-generated targeting. Data privacy and AI transparency are central to his technology-related platform.

sources.title: NIDDA - Matthew Winterhawk for Governor of Nevada (2026)

Election Reform.stance: Winterhawk supports election-integrity measures including mandatory voter ID, prohibiting non-citizen voting, ending universal mail ballots, requiring paper trails and independent audits, cleaning voter rolls, and county-level tabulation oversight.

sources.title: Top 6 Issues for Nevada Voters | Winterhawk on Education, Safety, Housing, Elections

Local Issues.stance: Winterhawk says Nevada land and water should remain under state control. He supports the Nevada Land Sovereignty and Reclamation Act, auditing federal water contracts, prioritizing agriculture and conservation, and increasing transparency in development approvals.

sources.title: Top 6 Issues for Nevada Voters | Winterhawk on Education, Safety, Housing, Elections
Nevada Land Sovereignty and Reclamation Act (NLSARA)

candidates.donor_summary: No campaign finance data found in public disclosures as of 2026-04-01. I found a Ballotpedia candidate page and a campaign/social profile, but no accessible OpenSecrets, FEC, or Nevada state finance page with donor totals for Matthew Winterhawk. Confidence: low. Public materials found in search results are limited and do not provide a documented voting history; the campaign site and Ballotpedia identify him as a gubernatorial candidate. Confidence: medium. Source URLs: https://ballotpedia.org/Matthew_Winterhawk ; https://www.nvgov2026.org/

candidates.name: Irina Hansen
candidates.summary: Irina Hansen is a Republican candidate for Nevada governor in 2026. She says she is a longtime real estate agent and salon owner, and her campaign says she is running on government accountability, affordability, public safety, education reform, and support for small businesses.

summary_sources.title: Irina Hansen announces candidacy for Nevada governor in 2026 race
Hansen's POSITIONS - Irina Hansen For Nevada Governor

candidates.donor_summary: Irina Hansen’s campaign finance picture was not clearly confirmed in the materials reviewed. The OpenSecrets link currently attached to the profile appears to be for Ira Hansen rather than Irina Hansen, so it should be treated cautiously until an Irina Hansen-specific Nevada campaign finance filing is verified.
candidates.voting_summary: Irina Hansen does not appear to have a legislative voting record under that name. Available campaign and reference materials identify her as a gubernatorial candidate and focus on her policy positions rather than any prior elected voting history.

links.title: Hansen's POSITIONS - Irina Hansen For Nevada Governor
Contributions by Geography 2021 - Ira Hansen - OpenSecrets

Economy.stance: Irina Hansen says she wants to strengthen the economy by supporting small businesses, reducing unnecessary regulation, and expanding access to capital through grants and microloans. Her campaign also proposes a Nevada Small Business Hub and fee reductions for some new entrepreneurs.

sources.title: Hansen's POSITIONS - Irina Hansen For Nevada Governor

Immigration.stance: Irina Hansen says she wants to end sanctuary policies, enforce the rule of law, and support legal immigrants by prioritizing their access to resources and services. Her campaign frames this as a combination of stricter enforcement and support for legal immigration.

sources.title: END SANCTUARY POLICIES - Irina Hansen for Nevada Governor
Hansen's POSITIONS - Irina Hansen For Nevada Governor

Guns & Safety.stance: Irina Hansen says she will defend the right of law-abiding citizens to keep and bear arms and opposes restrictions she views as infringing on Second Amendment rights. Her public safety platform also calls for stronger law-enforcement resources and tougher penalties for violent and repeat offenders.

sources.title: SECOND AMENDMENT - Irina Hansen For Nevada Governor
Hansen's POSITIONS - Irina Hansen For Nevada Governor

Election Reform.stance: Irina Hansen supports voter ID requirements, regular voter-roll audits, and banning ballot harvesting. She says these measures are intended to restore public trust in Nevada elections.

sources.title: Election Integrity - Irina Hansen For Nevada Governor
Hansen's POSITIONS - Irina Hansen For Nevada Governor

Local Issues.stance: Irina Hansen’s local-issues platform centers on affordable housing, anti-corruption enforcement, and government accountability. She says she would audit housing-fund fraud, streamline development, and tie funding to measurable results while also addressing homelessness through treatment, mental health support, and job training.

sources.title: Affordable Housing - Irina Hansen For Nevada Governor
Hansen's POSITIONS - Irina Hansen For Nevada Governor

Education.stance: Irina Hansen supports increasing funding for school choice, with education dollars following the student to public, charter, private, or homeschool options. She also says she supports expanding vocational and career training and giving parents more say in their children’s education.

sources.title: Education - Irina Hansen For Nevada Governor
Hansen's POSITIONS - Irina Hansen For Nevada Governor

Reproductive Rights.stance: Irina Hansen’s campaign materials reviewed here do not publish a direct position on abortion access or reproductive rights. Her public issue pages focus instead on education, public safety, immigration, election integrity, and constitutional rights more broadly.

sources.title: Hansen's POSITIONS - Irina Hansen For Nevada Governor

Climate/Energy.stance: Irina Hansen’s campaign materials reviewed here do not set out a detailed climate or energy policy. The available platform emphasizes government accountability, small business growth, public safety, education, and constitutional rights rather than emissions, renewables, or energy regulation.

sources.title: Hansen's POSITIONS - Irina Hansen For Nevada Governor

Tech & AI.stance: No specific public position on technology policy or artificial intelligence was identified in the campaign materials reviewed. Her published platform focuses on education, public safety, immigration, election integrity, and economic regulation.

sources.title: Hansen's POSITIONS - Irina Hansen For Nevada Governor

Social Justice.stance: Irina Hansen’s published platform does not include a specific social justice agenda. Her campaign materials instead emphasize constitutional rights, public safety, election integrity, and reducing government corruption and regulation.

sources.title: Hansen's POSITIONS - Irina Hansen For Nevada Governor

Foreign Policy.stance: Irina Hansen’s campaign materials reviewed here do not include a Nevada-specific foreign policy platform. Her public positions focus on state-level issues such as immigration enforcement, education, public safety, and economic regulation.

sources.title: Hansen's POSITIONS - Irina Hansen For Nevada Governor

Healthcare.stance: Irina Hansen’s campaign materials reviewed here do not set out a detailed healthcare policy. Her published platform focuses on government accountability, public safety, education, immigration, and economic issues rather than healthcare reform.

sources.title: Hansen's POSITIONS - Irina Hansen For Nevada Governor

candidates.name: Alexis Hill
candidates.summary: Alexis Hill is the chair of the Washoe County Commission and a Democratic candidate for Nevada governor in 2026. Her campaign emphasizes local-government experience and a platform focused on taxes, housing affordability, schools, infrastructure, and public services.

summary_sources.title: Washoe County Commissioner Alexis Hill explains why she's running for Nevada governor

candidates.donor_summary: Public campaign finance records for Alexis Hill are available through the Nevada Secretary of State. The campaign appears to be in an early reporting stage, so the available filings do not yet provide a complete picture of major donors or overall fundraising.
candidates.voting_summary: Alexis Hill is a county commissioner rather than a state legislator, so she does not have a legislative roll-call voting record to summarize. Her public record instead comes from Washoe County Commission actions and her campaign platform on taxes, housing, schools, infrastructure, and public safety.

links.title: Alexis Hill - NV Secretary of State Campaign Reporting
Democrat Alexis Hill jumps into Nevada governor race, unveils revenue-raising tax plan

Economy.stance: Hill says Nevada should modernize its tax system, end tax giveaways to billionaires and large corporations, and invest more in workforce development and local businesses rather than relying on subsidies for major outside firms. She frames this as a way to fund public services and create more stable long-term growth.

sources.title: Issues - Alexis Hill
No sacred cows in Hill’s Nevada
'Dark horse' Nevada governor candidate Alexis Hill fights to break through

Education.stance: Hill says Nevada should close its education funding gap and reach the national average, which she says would require about $2.5 billion in additional annual spending over the next decade. She supports universal pre-K, free school breakfast and lunch, higher teacher pay, and expanded workforce and career training.

sources.title: Issues - Alexis Hill
No sacred cows in Hill’s Nevada

Healthcare.stance: Hill says supporting families includes strengthening Nevada’s healthcare system and improving access to care. Her campaign presents healthcare as part of a broader affordability and family-support agenda rather than a standalone policy area.

sources.title: Issues - Alexis Hill

Immigration.stance: Hill says immigration policy should be handled at the federal level, opposes costly state-run detention centers, and does not support using the Nevada National Guard to indiscriminately round up immigrants. She says Nevada should partner with federal agencies, protect due process, and support comprehensive reform with pathways to citizenship.

sources.title: Issues - Alexis Hill
Is Nevada sanctuary state? See what new law says on access to schools

Foreign Policy.stance: Hill’s campaign does not present a distinct foreign-policy platform; the closest related position is her view that immigration enforcement should remain a federal responsibility and that Nevada should avoid creating its own detention or enforcement system. This is a state-level race, so her public statements are primarily on domestic policy.

sources.title: Issues - Alexis Hill

Election Reform.stance: Hill says Nevada should strengthen voter protections, protect voter information, and speed up the collection of election results. She also supports fair access to primary elections for registered nonpartisans.

sources.title: Issues - Alexis Hill

Local Issues.stance: Hill’s campaign focuses on housing affordability, road funding, homelessness, childcare, public safety, and park and infrastructure investment. She says state and local governments should fund these services sustainably rather than relying on temporary fixes or corporate subsidies.

sources.title: Issues - Alexis Hill

Reproductive Rights.stance: Hill says she is a staunch supporter of the right to choose and has said she was endorsed by Planned Parenthood Action in prior runs for office. Her campaign presents abortion access as part of a broader privacy and personal-freedom platform.

sources.title: Issues - Alexis Hill

Climate/Energy.stance: Hill says protecting Nevada’s air, water, land, and climate is fundamental to preserving quality of life, and she points to Washoe County’s sustainability planning as part of her record. Her campaign links environmental protection with long-term planning and public investment.

sources.title: Issues - Alexis Hill

Tech & AI.stance: Hill says Nevada should invest in modern digital infrastructure and update state systems on a regular schedule to better serve residents and protect information. Her campaign also highlights growth in sectors such as fintech, medtech, drones, and unmanned vehicles.

sources.title: Issues - Alexis Hill

Guns & Safety.stance: Hill says public safety is central to her platform and that Nevada needs sustainable funding for police, firefighters, and other first responders. Her campaign also ties safety to infrastructure, homelessness response, and community investment rather than only criminal-justice enforcement.

sources.title: Issues - Alexis Hill

Social Justice.stance: Hill says she will protect LGBTQ+ rights, support Native American sovereign rights and resources, and preserve privacy and dignity for individuals. Her campaign frames these positions as part of a broader human-rights agenda.

sources.title: Issues - Alexis Hill

flags.concern: Timestamp is set to 2026-04-01 (future date), and all source 'last_accessed' dates throughout the profile are also future-dated. This is a critical data integrity issue that calls into question when the profile was actually created and whether sources were genuinely accessed.
flags.suggestion: Correct to actual creation/access date. Verify all 'last_accessed' timestamps in source objects are accurate and historical.

flags.concern: Array contains 5 duplicate entries of the identical Emerson College survey (2025-11-18, 41%-41% Ford/Lombardo). Moreover, the poll completely excludes matchups with Alexis Hill and Matthew Winterhawk, despite the race description noting them as candidates. This creates a misleading picture of the actual competitive landscape.
flags.suggestion: Deduplicate to a single Emerson entry. Add polls from other reputable sources (Nevada Independent, Quinnipiac, etc.) that include additional candidate matchups to reflect the full field and recent polling trends.

flags.concern: Matthew Winterhawk's career_history array is empty, despite the summary claiming he is a 'father, husband, business operator, nonprofit founder.' The Ballotpedia source cited suggests some background exists but is not populated.
flags.suggestion: Research and add entries based on verified sources (campaign site, Ballotpedia, news articles). At minimum, document the nonprofit and business roles referenced in the summary.

flags.concern: Matthew Winterhawk's education array is empty. The summary mentions Johnson County Community College attendance, but no structured education entry exists, creating asymmetry with other candidates.
flags.suggestion: Add documented education entries from reliable sources. Include Johnson County Community College and any other verifiable degrees or certifications.

flags.concern: Aaron Ford's education array is poorly structured, with five entries for 'The Ohio State University' mixing Ph.D., Law degree, and null degrees with fields like 'Interdisciplinary studies' and 'International education.' This is confusing and appears to conflate degree fields with coursework or interests.
flags.suggestion: Consolidate into 3 clear entries: (1) Undergraduate degree in Interdisciplinary/International Education; (2) J.D./Law degree; (3) Ph.D. in Educational Administration. Verify years where possible from official sources.

flags.concern: The donor_source_url points to OpenSecrets for 'Ira Hansen,' not 'Irina Hansen.' The donor_summary explicitly acknowledges this mismatch as problematic, yet the URL is not corrected. This link appears to be for a different person entirely.
flags.suggestion: Remove the incorrect OpenSecrets link. Replace with a verified Nevada Secretary of State campaign finance filing for Irina Hansen, or explicitly state that campaign finance data is not yet publicly available if the filing exists but is not yet indexed.

flags.concern: Matthew Winterhawk's top_donors array is empty, but the donor_summary acknowledges data exists in Nevada SOS records. The asymmetry with other candidates (who have detailed donor lists) creates an impression of lesser support or transparency.
flags.suggestion: Research and populate with available donor data from Nevada Secretary of State filings if Winterhawk has filed. If no donors are disclosed yet, clarify that in the donor_summary.

flags.concern: Irina Hansen's issues section has only 5 substantive stances (Economy, Immigration, Guns & Safety, Election Reform, Local Issues), with 5 additional categories marked as 'medium' or 'low' confidence with minimal detail. This is incomplete compared to Lombardo (11 issues) and Ford (11 issues), potentially biasing perceptions of her platform depth.
flags.suggestion: Expand with research from her campaign website (irinahansenforgovernor.com), recent news interviews, or public statements on missing areas like Healthcare, Climate/Energy, Reproductive Rights, Tech & AI, and Social Justice.

flags.concern: Alexis Hill's issues section covers only 9 categories with several at low confidence (Foreign Policy, Social Justice), and is missing focused detail on Reproductive Rights, Tech & AI, and Guns & Safety that other candidates include. This creates an uneven comparative profile.
flags.suggestion: Research Hill's positions on missing or underdeveloped issues from her campaign site (alexisfornevada.com), Washoe County Commission records, and recent news coverage to ensure parity with other candidates.

flags.concern: Three secondary candidates (Winterhawk, Hansen, Hill) have empty or minimal career_history arrays, while Lombardo and Ford have 4 and 5 detailed entries respectively. This asymmetry may unintentionally signal lack of qualification or establishment credibility.
flags.suggestion: Populate career histories for all candidates based on their campaign bios, news profiles, and public records. Include titles, organizations, years, and brief descriptions consistent with the format used for Lombardo/Ford.

flags.concern: Matthew Winterhawk, Alexis Hill, and Irina Hansen have missing or null image_url values, while Lombardo and Ford have valid Wikipedia/Wikimedia URLs. This creates visual asymmetry in a profile that may be used for candidate comparison.
flags.suggestion: Search for official campaign photos or public domain images via campaign websites, news archives, or Wikimedia Commons. If unavailable, explicitly set to null with a note rather than omitting.

flags.concern: Both Lombardo and Ford have voting_record entries marked 'absent' with a vote date of '2026-03-29,' described as 'N/A' because they hold/held executive positions. This terminology is misleading—'absent' implies they were eligible to vote but didn't show up.
flags.suggestion: Modify the entry to use a clearer vote value like 'not_applicable' or 'no_record,' and update the bill_description to state 'Executive position—no legislative voting record' rather than framing as an absence.

flags.concern: The race description notes Alexis Hill as a candidate ('Washoe County Commission Chair Alexis Hill is also running, along with other candidates'), but the summary and early text focus heavily on Lombardo and Ford, potentially underrepresenting the full field and Hill's relevance in polling.
flags.suggestion: The description is accurate; however, ensure polling section includes Hill and Winterhawk matchups to align with the stated candidate field.

flags.concern: Climate policy coverage is asymmetric: Lombardo has low confidence ('No detailed platform'), while Ford has high confidence citing specific AG actions on EPA regulations. For a race where climate may matter to voters, Lombardo's position deserves deeper research.
flags.suggestion: Investigate whether Lombardo has made any public statements or executive actions on renewable energy, water conservation, or emissions. Check recent legislative sessions or press releases from gov.nv.gov.

flags.concern: The 'generator' field lists 5 AI model names, suggesting this profile was created or augmented by multiple LLM systems. This metadata is unusual for a fact-checking review and raises questions about consistency and source verification methodology.
flags.suggestion: Document the methodology used for profile generation and verification. If AI was used, clearly disclose which sections and for what purposes (research, formatting, drafting). Human review is essential for accuracy.

flags.concern: Source quality is strong overall (official government sites, Ballotpedia), but campaign URLs (joelombardofornv.com, fordfornevada.com) may change or be archived. Consider supplementing with Ballotpedia or Wikipedia links as permanent backups.
flags.suggestion: Retain current sources but add note that campaign URLs are volatile and subject to change; recommend periodic verification of links, especially as the race progresses.

reviews.summary: This profile demonstrates strong foundational work with comprehensive issue coverage, high-quality sourcing from official government and campaign sites, and generally neutral language. However, it suffers from critical data integrity issues that undermine reliability: future-dated timestamps throughout, duplicate polling entries, significant biographical gaps for secondary candidates (Winterhawk, Hansen, Hill), and an incorrect OpenSecrets link for Irina Hansen. The asymmetry in candidate coverage—detailed for Lombardo and Ford, sparse for others—may create an unintended bias toward viability perception. These issues require targeted fixes before publication.

flags.concern: The timestamp is set to a future date (2026-04-01), rendering all 'last_accessed' metadata chronologically impossible.
flags.suggestion: Correct all timestamps to reflect the actual date of generation.

flags.concern: The polling array contains four duplicate entries of the same survey, which inflates the apparent volume of data and degrades the quality of the dataset.
flags.suggestion: Remove duplicate entries and ensure only unique, distinct polls are listed.

flags.concern: The link provided leads to 'Ira Hansen' (a legislator) rather than 'Irina Hansen' (the candidate), indicating a high probability of data misattribution.
flags.suggestion: Verify the candidate's actual financial records with the Nevada Secretary of State or remove the incorrect link.

flags.concern: Career history and education fields are empty for Matthew Winterhawk, creating an unfair biographical disparity between him and other candidates.
flags.suggestion: Populate with publicly available biographical information or explicitly note the lack of verifiable data.

flags.concern: The education array for Aaron Ford is structurally redundant, listing the same institution multiple times for single degrees.
flags.suggestion: Consolidate to a single entry per degree and institution.

flags.concern: Alexis Hill's issues are notably less comprehensive than those of Joe Lombardo or Aaron Ford, missing critical categories like Reproductive Rights and Guns & Safety.
flags.suggestion: Conduct a thorough sweep of her campaign platform to provide a comparable level of issue coverage.

reviews.summary: While the profile provides a solid foundation for major candidates with high-confidence sourcing on policy stances, it suffers from significant data integrity and structural inconsistencies. Key issues include duplicated polling data, a critical misattribution of financial data for Irina Hansen, and noticeable gaps in biographical and issue coverage for lesser-known candidates compared to the incumbents.

flags.concern: The timestamp is set to a future date (2026-04-01), which is inconsistent with current data and undermines the profile's credibility.
flags.suggestion: Update to the actual date of profile generation or last modification to reflect real-time accuracy.

flags.concern: The array contains multiple identical entries for the same Emerson College poll, limiting diversity and potentially skewing perceptions of the race, while excluding other candidates or broader matchups.
flags.suggestion: Remove duplicates and incorporate additional polls from reputable sources to provide a more comprehensive and balanced view of voter sentiment.

flags.concern: The career history array for Matthew Winterhawk is empty, creating a gap in biographical completeness compared to other candidates.
flags.suggestion: Research and add verifiable details from sources like his campaign website or public records.

flags.concern: The education array for Matthew Winterhawk is empty, making it difficult to fairly assess his background.
flags.suggestion: Populate with available information from credible sources, or note if none exists.

flags.concern: Aaron Ford's education array has redundant entries for the same institution with null or unclear fields, reducing clarity and readability.
flags.suggestion: Consolidate into distinct entries with accurate degree, field, and year details based on verified sources.

flags.concern: The donor summary for Irina Hansen references incorrect data (e.g., linking to 'Ira Hansen' on OpenSecrets), which could misattribute financial information.
flags.suggestion: Verify and replace with accurate Nevada Secretary of State records specific to Irina Hansen.

flags.concern: Matthew Winterhawk's issues section is incomplete, with some stances marked as 'medium' confidence and lacking depth in certain areas compared to other candidates.
flags.suggestion: Expand with additional research from campaign materials to ensure consistent coverage across all candidates.

flags.concern: Irina Hansen's issues section omits key topics like Healthcare and Reproductive Rights, leading to uneven completeness relative to other profiles.
flags.suggestion: Add stances on missing issues using her campaign site or news sources for balanced representation.

reviews.summary: This profile offers a detailed and generally neutral overview of the 2026 Nevada gubernatorial race, with strong sourcing for major candidates like Joe Lombardo and Aaron Ford, but it requires revisions due to inconsistencies in timestamps, duplicated polling data, gaps in biographical details for lesser-known candidates, and uneven coverage of issues across profiles, which affect overall completeness and accuracy.

polling_note: Recent polling is limited. The most recent poll found in the last 90 days was Emerson College Polling’s November 16-18, 2025 survey showing Joe Lombardo and Aaron Ford tied at 41% each, with 18% undecided.
